task_completion.description: Verify that the 'PLATE' and 'SLOTS' layers are created with the correct colors.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Layer List widget (usually on the right side of the LibreCAD interface). You should see layers named 'PLATE' (with a white/black color indicator depending on theme) and 'SLOTS' (with a cyan color indicator).

task_completion.description: Verify that the base plate rectangle is drawn on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main drawing canvas. There should be a large white rectangle representing the 300x250 base plate.

task_completion.description: Verify that two slots are drawn on the left side of the plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the left half of the white rectangle on the canvas. There should be two cyan-colored slots, one near the bottom (y=50) and one near the top (y=200).

task_completion.description: Verify that two slots are drawn on the right side of the plate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the right half of the white rectangle on the canvas. There should be two cyan-colored slots, one near the bottom (y=50) and one near the top (y=200), mirroring the left side.

task_completion.description: Verify that the slots have the correct obround shape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the shape of the cyan slots. Each must clearly be an obround shape: consisting of a left semicircle, a right semicircle, and straight horizontal lines connecting their top and bottom edges.

task_completion.description: Verify that the drawing is saved with the correct filename and path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the LibreCAD window or a 'Save As' dialog box. The filename should be 'motor_base_plate.dxf' and the path should be '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking on LibreCAD tools, menus, or typing in the LibreCAD command line at the bottom of the screen.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use a script to generate the DXF file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen opening a terminal or text editor to write Python code (e.g., using ezdxf) to bypass manual drawing.

integrity.description: Verify that the drawing was constructed step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show intermediate states of the drawing (e.g., just the rectangle, then adding arcs, then lines) rather than the final completed drawing appearing instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: The expected output is a DXF file saved at '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/motor_base_plate.dxf'. The drawing requires a 'PLATE' layer (Color 7 / White) containing a 300x250 rectangle, and a 'SLOTS' layer (Color 4 / Cyan) containing four obround slots. Each slot has a radius of 7mm and a center-to-center length of 70mm (overall length 84mm). The slots are located at arc centers: (50,50) to (120,50); (50,200) to (120,200); (180,50) to (250,50); and (180,200) to (250,200).
